name: Input: any msg  |  Output: ctrlstate / opmode / speed / rapid / x,y,z / j1-j6
{ j1, j2, j3, j4, j5, j6 } degrees
HOME: robot moves to saved home position  |  SETHOME: saves current pose as the new home
Controls motor power via RWS  |  Override: msg.payload.action = 'motoron' | 'motoroff'
Moves TCP relative to current pose  |  X/Y/Z = base frame mm (max 50)  |  RX/RY/RZ = tool frame deg (max 30)
Rotates one joint by a relative angle (max 30 deg)  |  Override: msg.payload.{joint,dir,step}

Read current pose via RWS and save it as a named point  |  Storage: Local (points.json) or On-Robot (JSON file on the robot's own disk via RWS, no local file needed)  |  Go to a saved point: TCP Socket group → Go To Point  |  List/Delete saved points: Local group → Point List & Delete

Run Sequence: visits each saved point in order  |  Output 1 = after each step  |  Output 2 = done/stopped  |  Stop: halts after current step  |  Left: Storage=Local  |  Right: Storage=On-Robot (fetches the whole list once per run, not once per step)
PING: connectivity test  |  MOVEJ: absolute joint target  |  GRIP: gripper on/off via RWS /set-value (needs Access:All on the target DO)  |  ZONE: blend radius  |  STOP: halt motion
{ ok, variable, value, source, stale?, warning? }

SYSTEM-INFO: RW version + ctrl identity  |  ELOG: controller event log  |  FILE-READ: download file  |  UPLOAD-MOD: deploy RAPID .mod  |  RAPID-TASKS: list RAPID tasks + modules loaded on the controller

IO-LIST: enumerate all signals  |  DI-READ: digital in  |  DO-WRITE: digital out via RWS POST .../set-value — needs an I/O board (e.g. DSQC1030) with the signal's Access Level set to All in RobotStudio, else 403
Enable/disable robot lead-through (hand-guiding) via RWS  |  Requires manual mode + enable switch held on FlexPendant
EXPORT: dump saved-points list to msg.payload  |  IMPORT: replace list from msg.payload array (connect Export output to Import input for backup/restore)
STATE/IO: WebSocket push from controller  |  VAR/POSE: polling (toggle on/off with any input)  |  Messages stream to debug until toggled off or redeployed
{ ok, variable, value, source, stale?, warning? }
{ ok, x, y, z, q1, q2, q3, q4 }

⚠ DO Write needs an I/O board (e.g. DSQC1030 Scalable I/O) with the target signal's Access Level set to All in RobotStudio (Controller → Configuration → I/O System → Signal) — otherwise RWS rejects the write with 403. No analog I/O exists on this controller (gofa-ai-read/gofa-ao-write were removed).

RAPID-EXEC: start / stop / resetpp / loadmod / activate the RAPID program  |  Requires Remote Start/Stop UAS grant (RobotStudio → Edit User Accounts) + Auto mode + Motors on  |  resetpp, loadmod, and activate acquire edit mastership automatically  |  loadmod/activate require RAPID to be STOPPED (confirmed live — HTTP 403 while running) — stop first, then loadmod/activate, then start again
{ ok, action, task, modulePath, module } or { ok:false, error }
{ ok, action, task, module } or { ok:false, error }
{ ok, variable, value, source, stale?, warning? }

SPEED: override 1–100%  |  GETVAR/SETVAR: read/write a RAPID PERS variable by name — built-in test vars: nTestVar (num), sTestMsg (string)  |  bStopMotion below is NOT allow-listed — demonstrates the stale module-text fallback
{ ok, variable, value, source:"module-text", stale:true, warning }
Look up a saved point by name/id and move to it via GOTOJ/GOTOL  |  msg.payload.moveType: "J" (default, joint-interpolated) or "L" (linear)  |  Left: Storage=Local  |  Right: Storage=On-Robot
SETLED: color + optional hardware blink period  |  RESETLED: restore default solid green  |  blinkCount>0 runs a software-timed blink instead of the hardware period
List all saved points, or remove one by name/id  |  Left column: Storage=Local, points.json on the Node-RED host  |  Right column: Storage=On-Robot, JSON file on the robot's own disk via RWS

Start/Stop EGM via the Action dropdown (bare date-payload injects, same pattern as gofa-motor's Motors ON/OFF). Sine-sweep function node feeds a live target into gofa-egm-move, which forwards it to the active session (output 1) or falls back to a normal gofa-movej TCP move (output 2) if EGM isn't running.
Sine sweep joint 6 (±3°, 8s) -> feeds into gofa-egm-move
